name: Laryngeal Paralysis
description: Laryngeal paralysis occurs when the nerves controlling the cartilage flaps at the larynx fail, preventing the airway from opening fully during inspiration. This causes progressive breathing difficulty and is often part of a wider polyneuropathy in older large-breed dogs.
treatment_overview: Surgical arytenoid lateralisation ('tie-back' procedure) significantly improves breathing. Aspiration pneumonia is the main post-operative risk. Avoid heat and excitement in all cases.
prevention_tips: No proven prevention for acquired form
